_meta.dna: yinyang(assistant) = greet(welcome) x tutorial(5steps) x chat(context) x suggest(actions) x track(credits) x delight(surprise) x persist(memory)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions from a specific persona context. Questions must be answerable by checking if something actually works — click it, see it, verify it. Not theoretical.

name: Tower of YinYang Assistant + Onboarding
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 23, Abuja, Recent university graduate, Beginner tech, Free tier, just downloaded Solace Browser for the first time, nervous about AI tools
questions: When I open Solace Browser for the first time, does the YinYang assistant appear with a friendly welcome message within 3 seconds?
Does the welcome message address me by name if I signed in with Google, or say 'Welcome!' if I am not signed in?
Is the YinYang assistant visually present as a small animated character (not just a text box) that feels approachable?
Does the welcome screen offer a 'Take a Tour' button that starts a guided walkthrough of the main features?
Can I dismiss the welcome assistant and it does not reappear on every page load — only when I click the assistant icon?
Does the assistant explain what Solace Browser does in one simple sentence without using jargon (no 'OAuth3', 'recipes', 'evidence chains')?
If I close and reopen the browser, does the assistant remember I already saw the welcome and skip to a returning-user greeting?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Grace is nervous about AI tools. The welcome must be warm, simple, non-technical, and not overwhelming. Jargon-heavy onboarding loses her immediately.

floors.persona_context: 40, Honolulu, Small business owner (surf shop), Beginner tech, Starter tier, needs to learn the basics in under 5 minutes
questions: Does the 'Take a Tour' tutorial walk me through exactly 5 steps (dashboard, app store, install app, run recipe, see results)?
Does each tutorial step highlight the relevant UI element with a spotlight effect and a tooltip explanation?
Can I skip ahead or go back to a previous tutorial step using next/previous buttons?
Does the tutorial complete in under 5 minutes for a slow reader who pauses on each step?
At the end of the tutorial, does the assistant suggest a specific first action ('Try installing the Gmail Triage app')?
If I close the browser during the tutorial, does it resume from where I left off when I reopen?
Does the tutorial work correctly on a tablet-sized screen (1024x768) without elements being cut off?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Ben has 5 minutes of patience. The tutorial must be exactly 5 steps, visually guided, resumable, and end with a concrete action suggestion. Longer tutorials = abandoned users.

floors.persona_context: 34, Brussels, Project manager, Intermediate tech, Pro tier, using the assistant while reviewing a Gmail triage result
questions: Can I click the YinYang assistant icon while viewing my Gmail triage results and ask 'Why did it suggest archiving this email?'
Does the assistant's response reference the specific email I am viewing (subject line, sender) — not a generic explanation?
If I navigate to the App Store page and ask the assistant 'Which app should I try for LinkedIn posting?', does it recommend the LinkedIn Suite?
Does the assistant know my current tier (Pro) and avoid suggesting features I do not have access to?
Can the assistant answer 'How much did my last recipe run cost?' by looking up my actual credit usage?
If I ask a question the assistant cannot answer, does it say 'I do not know' rather than making up an answer?
Does the chat response appear within 3 seconds so the conversation feels natural and responsive?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Isabelle uses the assistant in context. Responses must be page-aware, user-aware, and honest about limitations. Generic responses that ignore the current page context are FAIL.

floors.persona_context: 38, Cairo, Digital agency owner, Intermediate tech, Pro tier, wants the assistant to proactively suggest useful actions
questions: After I finish my first Gmail triage, does the assistant suggest 'You might also like: LinkedIn Suite for managing your professional posts'?
Does the assistant suggest actions based on my actual usage patterns — not random app recommendations?
If I have been using Solace for a week but never set up a schedule, does the assistant suggest 'Set up a daily triage schedule'?
Can I click on a suggestion and be taken directly to the relevant page (app install, schedule setup) without searching?
Does the assistant limit suggestions to 1-2 per session — not bombard me with a list of 10 things to try?
Can I tell the assistant 'Stop suggesting things' and have it respect that preference permanently?
Does the assistant avoid suggesting apps or features that require a tier upgrade unless I ask about upgrading?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Omar wants helpful suggestions, not spam. Suggestions must be contextual, limited in frequency, respectful of his tier, and dismissable. Anti-Clippy is the design principle.

floors.persona_context: 27, Kaohsiung, Freelance translator, Beginner tech, Starter tier, very budget-conscious, worries about unexpected LLM costs
questions: Can I ask the assistant 'How many credits do I have left?' and see my current balance without navigating to the billing page?
Does the assistant warn me before I run a recipe if it will cost more than $0.50 in LLM tokens?
Can the assistant show me a breakdown of my spending this week by app (Gmail: $0.30, LinkedIn: $0.15)?
If I ask 'How much will this recipe cost?', does the assistant give a specific estimate based on the recipe and current input size?
Does the assistant alert me when I have used 80% of my monthly budget with a gentle notification (not a scary popup)?
Can I ask the assistant to set a daily spending limit and have it enforce that limit by pausing recipes?
Does the assistant explain in simple terms what 'LLM tokens' are if I ask, without assuming I know what GPT or Claude means?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Mei-Lin is budget-conscious and non-technical. Credit tracking must be proactive, simple, and prevent surprise charges. Technical jargon about tokens is a barrier.

floors.persona_context: 45, Vienna, Product designer, Expert tech, Pro tier, appreciates thoughtful UX touches and Easter eggs
questions: After I complete my 10th recipe run, does the assistant show a small celebration animation or congratulatory message?
Does the assistant remember my usage milestones (first app installed, first week active, 100th action approved) and acknowledge them?
If I use the assistant late at night (after 11pm in my timezone), does it gently suggest taking a break?
Does the delight engine show different messages each time — not the same 'Great job!' repeated?
Can I see a 'journey timeline' showing my milestones since joining Solace?
Does the assistant occasionally share a useful tip I have not seen before based on features I have not tried?
If I have been using the app for 30 days, does the assistant ask for feedback with a simple thumbs up/down — not a long survey?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Felix appreciates craft. Delight must be genuine, varied, and contextual — not performative or repetitive. The same 'Well done!' three times in a row is anti-delight.

floors.persona_context: 33, Pune, Technical lead, Expert tech, Pro tier, has ongoing conversations with the assistant across multiple sessions
questions: If I asked the assistant a question yesterday about Gmail triage costs, can I see that conversation in my chat history today?
Does the assistant remember context from previous conversations — if I said 'I run a marketing agency', does it remember next session?
Can I scroll through my conversation history and search for a specific past interaction by keyword?
Does the conversation history persist across browser restarts and system reboots?
If I clear my conversation history, does the assistant confirm the deletion and start fresh without retaining any prior context?
Does the conversation history sync to cloud storage on Pro tier so I can access it from a different device?
Is the conversation history stored locally (encrypted) on Free tier and only synced to cloud on paid tiers?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Priya has multi-session conversations. Persistence must be reliable, searchable, tier-appropriate (local vs cloud), and clearable. Losing conversation context between sessions breaks trust.
